keywords.serpWeakness: Top results are basic calculators from gardening blogs or Home Depot/Gardener's Supply, lacking compaction factors, no price estimates, and poor mobile UX.
userComplaints: estimates are too low because they ignore soil settling and compaction
only works for rectangular beds, not curves or circles
no option to compare bulk vs bagged delivery costs

keywords.keyword: soil volume calculator for raised bed
keywords.serpWeakness: Most tools don't let you specify bed height in inches and then convert to cubic yards; old websites with lots of ads.
userComplaints: inputs reset if I change from feet to inches
doesn't tell me how many bags of soil to buy
ad-heavy pages crash on mobile

keywords.keyword: how much dirt do I need
keywords.serpWeakness: Top results are static articles, not interactive calculators; weak on step-by-step guidance for irregular shapes.
userComplaints: I have to download a separate app or fill a long form to get a number
results are in cubic yards but I need bag counts too
does not consider different soil types like topsoil vs garden blend

keywords.serpWeakness: Existing calculators are buried on landscaping company sites, often not mobile optimized and require ZIP code before showing results.
userComplaints: asks for my email to email me the ‘free’ estimate
can’t calculate for multiple areas at once
interface is from 2010 and doesn't load on phone

keywords.serpWeakness: Few dedicated tools; most are just contact forms for landscaping companies, not instant calculators.
userComplaints: doesn't include delivery fees
no comparison with bagged soil from big box stores
prices are outdated or hidden behind a quote request

keywords.serpWeakness: No modern tool that combines volume with amendment ratios (compost, perlite, peat); old agriculture extension pages dominate.
userComplaints: only calculates total volume, not how much of each amendment I need
doesn't ask what I'm planting so the mix ratio may be wrong
unit conversions are broken on mobile

keywords.serpWeakness: Existing tools are either simple volume calculators or recipe blogs; no interactive mix designer with real-time bag counts.
userComplaints: want a calculator that spits out a shopping list
mixes are given in parts but I need actual cubic feet per amendment
none of them handle multiple beds at once

keywords.serpWeakness: Generic construction calculators rank, not garden-specific; they don't factor in soil types or compaction.
userComplaints: assumes my garden is a perfect rectangle, no curves
no option to add 10% overage for settling
doesn't explain why cubic yards matter for gardening

keywords.serpWeakness: Many tools only do rectangular containers; round pots require manual math, and no bag count for different sizes.
userComplaints: round pots are a pain to calculate
I need to know how many 1.5 cu ft bags for my 10-inch pot collection
calculator doesn't remember my pot dimensions when I switch shapes

keywords.serpWeakness: Often tied to local delivery services, not usable as a standalone estimator; weak on visual aids.
userComplaints: forces me to pick a delivery date before showing the volume
no visual diagram of the area I'm filling
can't handle irregular shapes without doing math myself

keywords.keyword: how many bags of soil do I need
keywords.serpWeakness: Static tables dominate SERPs; no interactive calculator that adjusts for bag size and compaction.
userComplaints: bags come in different sizes but tables only show one brand
doesn't account for fluffing or settling of soil
have to calculate total cubic feet separately then convert

keywords.serpWeakness: Combined calculators are rare; users need to switch between separate tools or sites.
userComplaints: I need mulch depth in one and soil volume in another, should be unified
mulch calculators often use 3-inch depth but I use 4
no side-by-side cost comparison between mulch and soil

keywords.serpWeakness: Very niche; SERP is thin, mostly blog posts with simple formulas. No tool optimizes for vegetable root depth.
userComplaints: doesn't adjust depth for root vegetables vs leafy greens
no companion planting spacing impact on soil volume
missing a printable guide for amendments per planting zone

keywords.serpWeakness: Very weak competition; most are old contact form pages, no instant live pricing integration.
userComplaints: have to call and get spam calls later
won't show delivery fee until checkout
no way to compare multiple local suppliers in one place

keywords.serpWeakness: Pages are either about composting bin size or general soil volume; no calculator that tells how much compost to mix into existing soil.
userComplaints: I need to know how many bags of compost to amend my 200 sq ft garden
existing tools don’t let me set my existing soil quality level
results don't include a recommended ratio for different soil types
